subject: Re: Incomplete Boxnet Retraining
body:
Hey Kyle,
The BoxNet model learns to classify every pixel as either particle, background or high-contrast dirt to erase - you are currently telling it your fiducials are particles not high contrast dirt to erase and should instead paint over them with the masking tool
Good luck!
Alister
 
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Feb 7, 2024, at 09:32, 'Kyle Messina' via Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:
﻿Currently I'm trying to retrain Boxnet to mask fiducials in my tilt series prior to subtomo averaging but something odd is going on during retraining.  Every time I try to retrain the Boxnet models, the results look similar to the attached images.  The accuracy metric barely moves to the right and either stays static or barely decreases.  I've tried multiple combinations of Boxnet models, in/excluding the default training images, and using different sets of manually selected particles.  All combinations result in an incomplete retraining to different extents with no hint as to why.  There is also no error produced to my knowledge.
If its relevant, I'm attempting to retrain the Boxnet models because the default Boxnets do not seem to work on my current set of micrographs.  I suspect its likely due to the high defocus of the dataset but I could be wrong since I'm still new to Warp.  Any advice would be appreciated, thank you!
 




--
